name: Thaye Ghost
description: The Thaye Ghost, a fearsome entity in Burmese folklore, is a vengeful spirit born from a violent or unjust death. Often female, though male Thaye exist, they are driven by a burning desire for retribution against those who wronged them in life. They are not simply malevolent; their actions are fueled by unresolved trauma and a desperate need to right past wrongs. Thaye Ghosts are known for their ability to manipulate the environment, causing objects to move, creating chilling whispers, and inducing vivid nightmares. They possess a profound connection to the location of their death, often haunting it relentlessly. While their primary goal is revenge, they can also be drawn to individuals who remind them of their tormentors or who possess similar characteristics to those who caused their suffering. These ghosts are powerful and dangerous, demanding respect and caution from those who encounter them. They represent the enduring impact of trauma and the complexities of justice and vengeance in the spirit world. The Thaye's appearance and behavior are often linked to the circumstances of their death, reflecting the pain and suffering they endured. Their story serves as a cautionary tale about the consequences of injustice and the enduring power of the spirit world in Burmese belief.
subtitle: Vengeful spirit of injustice.
appearance: Thaye Ghosts typically appear as they did in the moments before their death, often bearing the marks of their suffering. A female Thaye might have long, disheveled hair, tattered clothing, and a pale, gaunt face etched with sadness and anger. Male Thaye may appear equally disheveled, often bearing visible wounds or marks related to their demise. Their eyes are often described as burning with an intense, otherworldly light, reflecting the burning desire for revenge that consumes them. Some accounts describe them as having an ethereal, translucent quality, while others portray them as solid and tangible, capable of physically interacting with the world around them.
habitat: Thaye Ghosts primarily haunt the location of their death or the places closely associated with their suffering. This could be a home, a battlefield, a forest, or any other site where they experienced trauma. They are also drawn to places where injustice and suffering persist, seeking to alleviate or avenge the pain they witness. Temples or locations of great spiritual power may offer some respite, but ultimately, their connection to the site of their trauma remains the strongest.
behavior: Thaye Ghosts are driven by a relentless desire for revenge. Their behavior is often erratic and unpredictable, fueled by their unresolved trauma and anger. They may manifest as subtle disturbances, such as whispers and cold spots, or as more dramatic displays of telekinetic activity and emotional manipulation. They often target those who wronged them in life or individuals who remind them of their tormentors. While their primary goal is revenge, they may also attempt to communicate their story and seek justice for their suffering. Their behavior is a reflection of their pain and a desperate attempt to find peace in the afterlife.
weaknesses: Offering merit (good deeds) to the deceased

keyMyths.title: The Unjust Execution
keyMyths.description: A woman wrongly accused of a crime is executed. Her spirit returns as a Thaye, haunting the village and seeking revenge on those who falsely accused her, until a monk performs rituals to appease her.

sources.author: Maung Htin Aung
sources.description: A collection of traditional Burmese folk tales, including stories of ghosts and spirits.
sources.text: A collection of traditional Burmese folk tales, including stories of ghosts and spirits.
